parts.answer: The only correct answer is B (the mixture becomes more purple) A is incorrect because although there is no change in position of equilibrium the increase in pressure results in the purple iodine molecules being closer together so the colour becomes more intense C is incorrect because iodine vapour is not brown D is incorrect because iodine vapour is not brown (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because a change in pressure does not affect the value of K p C is incorrect because only temperature has an effect on the value of K p and an increase will result in K p decreasing and not increasing D is incorrect because temperature does have an effect on the value of K p (1) Number Answer Mark B is incorrect because the increase in the value of K p indicates a shift in the position of equilibrium to the products C is incorrect because the change in the value of K p does indicate a shift in the extent of reaction D is incorrect because the change in the value of K p is insufficient to result in almost complete conversion (1) Number Answer Mark 11 The only correct answer is B (13.53) A is incorrect because this is the pH determined using the value of pK w at 298 K C is incorrect because this is the value of pK w minus the concentration D is incorrect because this is the value of pK w (1) Number Answer Mark 12 The only correct answer is D (the An answer that makes reference to the following points: • iodine / I and alkali / NaOH / KOH • yellow precipitate for pentan-2-one and no change for pentan-3-one (1) (1) Accept alkaline iodine Allow iodoform reaction/test/result or iodoform/CHI / triiodomethane Allow solid/ppt/ppte for precipitate Allow antiseptic smell Allow only pentan-2-one forms a yellow precipitate Allow no result/no observation for no change M2 dependent on M1 but allow TE if alkali missing (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes reference to the following points: • Fehling’s/ Benedict’s/ Tollens’/acidified dichromate((VI)) • positive observation (for pentanal) which matches chosen test and no change (negative for pentan-2-one) (1) (1) Allow ammoniacal silver nitrate for Tollens’ Allow Cr O 2− and H + Ignore references to heat/reflux Do not award hydrochloric acid (Fehling’s or Benedict’s) brick-red/red/brownish red and ppt(from blue solution) (Tollens’) silver mirror Allow black/grey ppt (acidified dichromate(VI))goes green/blue (from orange) Allow no result/no observation for no change M2 dependent on M1 but allow TE for minor errors such as omission of acid for the dichromate Allow (1) for addition of 2,4-DNPH and comparison of melting temperatures of products (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • 2,4-dinitrophenylhydrazine/ 2,4-DNPH • the solids need to be recrystallised • their melting temperatures determined and compared with data/reference books (1) (1) (1) Allow lower case and minor misspellings such as 2,4-dinitrophenolhydrazine Allow Brady’s reagent Allow 2,4-DNP/2,4-dnp Ignore the colour given with the ppt Accept suitable descriptions of recrystallisation Allow purified and crystalised for recrystallisation Ignore just crystallisation without some qualification Do not award if the melting temperature is clearly stated to be that of pentan-2-one/ pentane-3-one /pentanal Do not award reference to boiling temperature(s) (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • the mechanism proceeds by nucleophilic/CN − attack on the planar carbonyl group • which is (equally/50:50 to) either side/ both sides/ above and below (and so results in a racemic mixture) • pentan-2-one product has a chiral carbon/carbon with four different groups attached and (forms) two optical isomers • pentan-3-one product is symmetrical/has two ethyl groups and so does not produce a product with a chiral carbon/optical isomer (1) (1) (1) (1) Marking points can be scored for correct labelled

• calculation of enthalpy change value • sign and units (1) (1) Example of calculation ∆H = ( − ∆S surroundings x T = − − 234.9 x 298=) ∆H = + 70000.2 / 70000 / 7.0 x 10 J mol −1 or ∆H = + 70.002 / 70 / 7.0 x 10 kJ mol −1 Ignore SF Allow J / mol or kJ / mol for units If two answers are given then both must be correct Correct answer without working scores (2) Allow M2 for incorrect value provided the units match the value, e.g. use of 25 instead of 298 gives +5872.5 J mol −1 or 5.8725 kJ mol −1 (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

• enthalpy of solution labelled arrow shown to go up from solid NaHCO • lattice energy gaseous species and labelled arrow to go down • enthalpy of hydration labelled arrow to go down and both aqueous ions species (1) (1) (1) Example of sketch Accept two labelled arrows if the hydration enthalpies for the ions are written separately Allow just singular ∆ hyd H for hydration enthalpy of both ions Accept +18.6 for ∆ sol H Allow ∆H hyd for ∆ hyd H and ∆H sol for ∆ sol H Allow any lengths of arrows (3) Section C Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

• molar mass of ethanoic acid • calculation of atom economy (1) (1) 2 × 12 + 2 × 16 + 4 = 60 Accept molar mass of 2-oxobutanedioic acid + water = 132 + 18 Allow 150 for the denominator Atom economy = (90 ÷ 150 × 100 =) 60 (%) Final answer without working scores (2) TE on incorrect molar mass e.g. use of 57 to give 61.2% scores (1) (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • (reagents) potassium dichromate((VI))/ K Cr O and sulfuric acid/ H SO • (heat under) reflux (1) (1) Accept sodium dichromate((VI))/ Na Cr O Ignore [O] Allow Cr O 2− and H + / acidified potassium dichromate((VI)) Ignore concentration if given If name and formula given then both must be correct Do not award hydrochloric acid Do not award M1 if any other reagents given Standalone mark (2) Number Answer Additional
